steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must explain a specific finding: both noise conditions (ship sounds alone vs. ship sounds + sonic pulses) significantly reduced narwhal diving, but the difference between those two conditions was negligible. The answer must explain why adding sonic pulses on top of ship sounds had almost no additional effect.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — sonic pulses can be heard at significantly greater ocean depths than ship sounds can.: If sonic pulses carry further, we would expect them to have a larger effect on behavior, not a negligible one. ✗
  • Choice B — the narwhals weren't as sensitive to human-caused sounds as the researchers had predicted.: Both conditions produced significant decreases in diving. That contradicts insensitivity. ✗
  • Choice C — ship sounds contribute so much to the overall sound level that the addition of sonic pulses has little effect on the narwhals' auditory environment.: If ship sounds already dominate the soundscape, adding pulses barely changes what the narwhals hear — explaining why the two conditions produce nearly identical behavior. ✓
  • Choice D — narwhals forage at shallower depths in the presence of ship sounds alone than in the presence of ship sounds coupled with sonic pulses.: This would predict a difference between conditions, but the passage says differences were negligible. ✗
That matches Choice C — ship sounds contribute so much to the overall sound level that the addition of sonic pulses has little effect on the narwhals' auditory environment..
